headline: AI and tech stocks tumble again
detail: Another day of broad selling in AI and tech names sets the tone directly against your book. PLTR, AVGO, CRWD, and your ETF holdings all live in this crossfire - a sustained down day compounds your existing losses, especially on margin.

headline: SpaceX and space stocks getting punished
detail: The headline explicitly calls out space stocks getting hit across the board as SpaceX FOMO fades. You hold both SPCX (new IPO, very limited data) and SPAL (2x leveraged SpaceX ETF that decays over time) - leveraged and brand-new names in a sector actively selling off is a dangerous mix on margin.

headline: Friday is a massive volume day on Wall Street
detail: Described as a roster-cut day with one of the highest volume sessions of the year, Friday can cause sharp, exaggerated price swings. For a margin account concentrated in volatile tech and chip names, this raises the real risk of stop-outs or fast moves that are hard to react to in time.

headline: Oil slides 2% on supply outlook
detail: Falling oil is generally a mild positive for tech and chip companies as it eases input and energy costs. Not a direct mover for your holdings, but it signals broader macro caution and risk-off sentiment that tends to weigh on growth names like yours.
